title: M01 Mastery Check — System Design Before Code
instructions: Complete both tasks. You pass this module when you can produce both outputs from memory without referencing lesson material.

tasks.title: Derive the Stack from Constraints
tasks.prompt: Given only these three requirements: (1) real-time collaborative editing in the browser, (2) enterprise multi-tenancy enforced below the application layer, (3) semantic search without an external service — derive the Meridian technology choices that each constraint forces. For each constraint, name the constraint, the forced technology, and at least one alternative it eliminates.
success_criteria: Constraint 1 → Lexical + Yjs CRDT → eliminates Next.js/SSR frameworks
Constraint 2 → PostgreSQL RLS → eliminates application-layer multi-tenancy approaches
Constraint 3 → pgvector extension → eliminates Pinecone, Weaviate, external vector DBs

tasks.title: Write an ADR
tasks.prompt: Write a complete Architecture Decision Record for Meridian's choice of Drizzle ORM over Prisma. Your ADR must include: Context, Decision, Rationale (with at least two reasons), and Consequences (what it commits you to).
success_criteria: Context section explains the ORM selection problem
Decision clearly states Drizzle is chosen
Rationale includes TypeScript-first design and no code generation step
Consequences section mentions what trade-offs are accepted (e.g., less ecosystem tooling than Prisma)
